_comment: Manifest describing one course's source materials to ingest. Copy to corpus.json, point paths at YOUR own materials (plain-text extracts of slides/notes), and run: curriculum build corpus.json. Paths may be absolute or relative to the working directory. token is a short stable id used as the grounding citation for that source.

sources._comment: Set spine:true on a source whose ordering is human-vetted (e.g. a textbook's chapter sequence). Its concepts are chained into trusted prerequisite edges (confidence 1.0) in document order; LLM inference may only add lower-confidence cross-links and can never overwrite these.

A 'procure' instruction declares a slot you still have to fill: while its file is missing, corpus-validate reports the source as pending (a warning, not an error) and build skips it, so a partially procured corpus still builds. Drop the extracted text at 'path' and rebuild; the note is inert once the file exists.
sources.procure: Extract chapter 9 of the course textbook (you own it) to plain UTF-8 text.
